meta.title: Is America's Red Sea defense really worth it?
meta.description:
Go to https://ground.news/Sandboxx to stay fully informed on military developments around the world. Subscribe through my link right now for 40% off their Vantage Plan, which is what I use everyday.

– BREAK –

Based on our calculations, the U.S. Navy has burned through roughly $780 million in ordnance defending commercial and military ships from Houthi attacks in the Red Sea over the past year or so, which is admittedly far more than the Houthis have spent on the attacks themselves. 

So let’s try to determine whether or not this effort is really worth the immense cost… and why these interceptors seem to cost so much.

https://www.csis.org/analysis/cost-and-value-air-and-missile-defense-intercepts
